title: Nostolachma triflorum
intro: Nostolachma triflorum, also known as Bael tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Nostolachma triflorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Nostolachma triflorum is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Nostolachma triflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Nostolachma, which belongs to the subfamily Ixoroideae. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as coffee or madder family. The Ixoroideae subfamily is one of the 12 subfamilies within the Rubiaceae family. Nostolachma triflorum is a tropical species native to Asia.
